title: El rascacielos latino
overview: Built against all the aesthetic canons of its time and inaugurated in 1923, the emblematic Buenos Aires building "Palacio Barolo" was commissioned by the Italian businessman Luis Barolo to a compatriot, the architect Mario Palanti, who would later build a "twin tower" on the other side of the Plata: the Palacio Salvo in Montevideo.
